Title: Arabidopsis thaliana alpha1,2-glucosyltransferase (ALG10) is required for efficient N-glycosylation and leaf growth

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UBQ10 promoter (constitutive) for complementation in Arabidopsis; ADH1 promoter (constitutive) for heterologous expression in Saccharomyces cerevisiae; transient ALG10-GFP expression construct in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in roots, stems and leaves; reduced in pollen, embryos and endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in roots, stems and leaves; reduced in pollen, embryos and endosperm
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycosyltransferase family GT59; multispanning membrane protein with predicted 12 transmembrane helices; three putative N-glycosylation sites
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Dolichol-linked oligosaccharide biosynthesis / N-glycosylation precursor assembly
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: ER lumenal terminal glucosylation step of lipid-linked oligosaccharide assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the terminal glucose residue in alpha-1,2 linkage from dolichol-P-glucose to Glc2Man9GlcNAc2-PP-dolichol, generating Glc3Man9GlcNAc2-PP-dolichol
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Endoplasmic reticulum membrane as lipid-linked oligosaccharide precursor; N-glycans on glycoproteins
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Required for efficient co-translational N-glycosylation, protein folding/quality control, normal leaf growth, and abiotic stress tolerance
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms the fully assembled lipid-linked precursor required for efficient transfer of N-glycans to nascent proteins
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant of complete precursor assembly; not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ALG10 is required for final assembly of the Glc3Man9GlcNAc2 lipid-linked precursor, efficient protein N-glycosylation, normal leaf growth, and salt/tunicamycin tolerance; loss causes accumulation of Glc2Man9GlcNAc2-PP-Dol and severe protein underglycosylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At5g02410 encodes the Arabidopsis ortholog of yeast ALG10 and localizes to the ER. 2. Arabidopsis ALG10 partially complements the Saccharomyces cerevisiae alg10 mutant, restoring Glc3Man9GlcNAc2 precursor formation and improving CPY glycosylation. 3. The alg10-1 null mutant lacks fully assembled Glc3Man9GlcNAc2-PP-Dol and accumulates mainly Glc2Man9GlcNAc2-PP-Dol. 4. alg10-1 shows severe glycoprotein underglycosylation, UPR activation, reduced leaf size, and increased salt and tunicamycin sensitivity. 5. UBQ10-driven ALG10 fully complements biochemical and growth phenotypes. 6. alg10-1 suppresses lethality/defects of alpha-glucosidase I mutants knf-14 and knf-101, consistent with ALG10 acting upstream of glucosidase I.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutant analysis; complementation in yeast alg10 mutant; complementation in Arabidopsis with UBQ10:ALG10; transient ALG10-GFP localization in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-ESI-MS analysis of lipid-linked oligosaccharides; immunoblotting/lectin blots for glycosylation defects; genetic interaction with knf-14 and knf-101
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene annotation data included with submission; gene expression profiles referenced from BAR and Genevestigator; no high-throughput omics accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: GnTI-CaaaTS-mRFP (co-localization marker only); STT3A; GCSI/KNF; CGL1/GnTI; PDI; TGG1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil growth; NaCl salt stress; tunicamycin treatment; ABA treatment; mannitol osmotic treatment; long-day conditions
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes smaller plants with altered leaf size, severe protein underglycosylation, ER stress/UPR activation, and increased salt/tunicamycin sensitivity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a mechanistic target to manipulate glycoprotein maturation and ER quality control, but not a favorable breeding target for crop improvement because loss of function impairs growth and stress tolerance; may be used experimentally to suppress alpha-glucosidase I deficiency or dissect glycosylation-dependent traits
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study focuses on protein glycosylation rather than classical nutritional metabolite accumulation. ALG10 acts in precursor assembly upstream of protein-bound N-glycan processing. Arabidopsis ALG10 lacks a C-terminal dilysine motif yet resides in the ER. The source species differs from heterologous validation species (yeast, Nicotiana benthamiana).
